finding: Slovak interest rate conditions reversed to tightening after prior quarter easing
bullets: A net 5% of Slovak firms (n=62) reported higher interest rates on bank financing — a reversal of 13.8 pp from the prior quarter's net easing of 8%, though still well below the EA's net 27% and DE's net 38% tightening, implying increased debt servicing costs for borrowers.

Non-interest financing costs (charges, fees, commissions) remained broadly stable, with a net 41% of Slovak firms (n=60) reporting tighter conditions — almost unchanged from the prior quarter's net 42% and approaching Germany's net 49%, putting pressure on firms' operational margins.

Credit availability eased marginally, with a net 6% of Slovak firms (n=62) reporting a reduction in the available size of loans or credit lines, compared with a net 7% in the previous quarter and the EA's net 9% — suggesting limited but persistent constraints on growth financing.
chart_subtitle: Net 5% of Slovak firms reported higher interest rates.
